title: Transport in Georgia (country)
content:
For Soviet transportation, see Transport in the Soviet Union. Railways. <br "total:" 1,683 km in common carrier service; does not include industrial lines <br "broad gauge:" 1,583 km of gauge (1993) <br "narrow gauge:" 100 km of gauge. City with <a href="rapid%20transit" metro</a system: <a href="Tbilisi" Tbilisi</a (see <a href="Tbilisi%20Metro" Tbilisi Metro</a). Highways.

The road network in Georgia consists of 1,603 kilometers of main or international highways that are considered to be in good condition and some 18,821 kilometers of secondary and local roads that are, generally, in poor condition. Only 7,854 km out of over 20,000 km of Georgian roads are paved. Motorways. Georgia has a small motorway system, that is currently under development, essentially when the motorway will be finished will link <a href="Tbilisi" Tbilisi</a, the capital, and <a href="Batumi" Batumi</a, Georgia's second largest city.

The multilane road is part of the <a href="S1%20highway%20%28Georgia%29" S1 highway</a, which runs from Mukhatgverdi (<a href="Tbilisi" Tbilisi</a West) until Agarebi, a village near <a href="Khashuri" Khashuri</a, and has a length of 110 km, bypassing <a href="Mtskheta" Mtskheta</a and <a href="Gori%2C%20Georgia" Gori</a. Pipelines.

<a href="petroleum" Crude oil</a 370 km; refined products 300 km; <a href="natural%20gas" natural gas</a 440 km (1992) Black Sea Ports and harbors. <a href="Batumi" Batumi</a, <a href="Poti" Poti</a, <a href="Sukhumi" Sokhumi</a, <a href="Kulevi%20Oil%20Terminal" Kulevi Oil Terminal</a Merchant marine.

<br "total:" 17 ships (with a volume of or over) totaling / <br "ships by type:" <a href="cargo%20ship" cargo ship</a 10, <a href="chemical%20tanker" chemical tanker</a 1, <a href="oil%20tanker" petroleum tanker</a 6 (1999 est.) Airports. 28 (1994 est.) In February 2007 a brand new, modern and fully equipped international Airport was inaugurated in Tbilisi. Airports - with paved runways.
